person.name: Sir John William Nicholas de Carter

nameForms.fullText: Sir John William Nicholas de Carter

person.alternateName: Sir John william nicholas de carter
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
William Carter of Watford (ca. 1460-1521) married Alice, maiden name unknown. William may have been a grandson of John Carter of Watford whose will of 14 December 1446 refers to five sons but not by name. William Carter signed his will on 14 August 1521; proved 30 August 1521. His son Richard was executor; the overseer was William Hyll. The will names three daughters (a) Alice and (b) Johanne, and three sons (c) Richard, (d) William of St. Albans and (e) John of Rickmansworth. Although Currer-Briggs included the will of William Carter of Watford in an appendix, he did not connect him in the narrative to the three sons noted above; but their inherited properties, clearly specified in the will, establish their identity beyond any doubt. 

John Carter of Rickmansworth, who left his will in 1545, had a son John Carter II, who also left his will in Hertfordshire in 1602. John II married (1) Joan Pyging and (2) Margery Dickinson. Of his six children, John Carter III (born 1564) married Elizabeth Hawtrey. They had two sons, both mariners who came to Virginia; James Carter died in England in 1626/27, while John Carter IV, who died at Sherley Hundred in Virginia in 1639, married (1) Jane Cleare in 1611 and (2) Joan Lee, widow of Thomas Lee the Vintner of Virginia. Cleare Carter, son of John Carter IV, was the father of Anne Carter who became the third wife of her third cousin once removed, Colonel John Carter of "Corotoman," #4, son of the Vintner John Carter of this discussion.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John William Nicholas de Carter於1475年出生在英國賀特福爾時，他的父親Sir John William Nicholas Carter of Cusworth25歲，母親Lady Margaret Frazier29歲。他於1490年與Martha Croxton在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1521年8月30日在英國英格蘭賀福爾享年46歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Carter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a transporter of goods, from Middle English carter(e) ‘carter’ (Anglo-Norman French car(e)tier, Old French charetier, medieval Latin carettarius, carettator). The Old French word coalesced with the earlier Middle English word cart(e) ‘cart’, which is from either Old Norse kartr or Old English cræt, both of which, like the Late Latin word, were probably derived from Celtic. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: shortened form of   McCarter  .
Americanized form of German   Karter   ‘carder’.

variants.name: Charman
meanings: English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for a carter, carrier, or coachman, from Middle English (Old French) char, car ‘car, cart, carriage’ + Middle English man ‘man’. Compare Carter and Carman 2.

English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for someone who did odd jobs, from Middle English char(re), chere (Old English cier(r), cer(r), cear(r), cær(r)) ‘turn (of activity), small job, chore’ + man. Compare Carman 2.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of Slovenian Čarman (see Carman 5).

variants.name: Chartier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Adelard, Alcide, Emile, Gilles, Raoul, Rosaire, Alphonse.
French: shortened form of charretier, an occupational name for a carter, from an agent derivative of Old French charette ‘cart’.
French Canadian: altered form of English   Carter  , a cognate of 1 above. Compare   Sharkey   and   Shorkey  .

variants.name: Karter
meanings: German: occupational name for a carder (someone who prepares wool for spinning), from Middle High German karter. Compare Carter .

variants.name: McCarter
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   McArthur  .

variants.name: Carrier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Cecile, Alain, Gaetan, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Normand.

English and southern French: from Middle English, Old French car(r)ier (from Late Latin carrarius, a derivative of carrum ‘cart, wagon’, a word of Gaulish origin), an occupational name for a carter (see Carter ), in French also for a cartwright. Compare French Charrier .
French: occupational name for a stonemason or quarryman, carrier. Compare Coyer .

variants.name: Cartman
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): occupational name from Middle English cartman ‘man who operated a cart for the transport of goods’. Compare Carter .
Americanized form of German Kartmann (see Kartman ).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

variants.name: Nicolas
meanings: From Late Greek. See Nicholas .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John William Nicholas de Carter was born in 1475, in Hertfordshire, England, his father, Sir John William Nicholas Carter of Cusworth, was 25 and his mother, Lady Margaret Frazier, was 29. He married Martha Croxton in 1490, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died on 30 August 1521, in Herefordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 46.

Il a épousé Martha Croxton en 1490, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé le 30 août 1521, à Herefordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 46 ans.

Он умер 30 августа 1521 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 46 лет.
